question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
## `charmcraft.yaml` Structure for a Kubernetes Charm

Here is a complete, annotated example of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes (sidecar) charm:

```yaml
# charmcraft.yaml

# ─── Identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
name: my-app                        # charm name (must match metadata.yaml if separate)
type: charm                         # always "charm" for operator charms

# ─── Metadata (can live here instead of a separate metadata.yaml) ─────────────
title: My Application
summary: A one-line summary of what this charm does.
description: |
  A longer description of the charm, its purpose,
  and any important details operators should know.

# ─── Bases / Platforms ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Declares which Ubuntu base(s) the charm is built and runs on.
bases:
  - build-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"
    run-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"

# ─── Containers (required for Kubernetes / sidecar charms) ───────────────────
containers:
  my-app:                           # container name referenced in the charm code
    resource: my-app-image          # must match a name under "resources" below
    mounts:
      - storage: data               # must match a name under "storage" below
        location: /var/lib/my-app   # mount path inside the container

# ─── Resources ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for my-app
    upstream-source: ghcr.io/my-org/my-app:latest   # default / upstream reference

# ─── Storage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent data directory
    minimum-size: 1G

# ─── Configuration options ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: INFO
      description: Log verbosity level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR)
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on

# ─── Relations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
requires:
  db:
    interface: pgsql
    limit: 1                        # only one PostgreSQL relation allowed

provides:
  http:
    interface: http

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-peer

# ─── Actions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  rotate-credentials:
    description: Rotate application credentials immediately.
    params:
      dry-run:
        type: boolean
        default: false
        description: If true, only report what would change.

# ─── Parts (how charmcraft builds the charm) ─────────────────────────────────
parts:
  my-app:
    plugin: python             # uses the charmcraft Python plugin
    source: .
    python-requirements:
      - requirements.txt
    build-packages:
      - libffi-dev
    stage-packages:
      - libffi8
```

---

## Key Points by Section

| Section | Required for K8s? | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `name` / `type` | ✅ Yes | Charm identity |
| `bases` | ✅ Yes | Build & runtime Ubuntu base |
| `containers` | ✅ Yes | Declares sidecar container(s) — **what makes it a K8s charm** |
| `resources` (oci-image) | ✅ Yes | OCI image resource referenced by containers |
| `storage` | Only if mounted | Persistent volumes for containers |
| `config` | No | User-facing configuration knobs |
| `requires` / `provides` / `peers` | No | Juju relation endpoints |
| `actions` | No | Operator runnable actions |
| `parts` | ✅ Yes | Build instructions for `charmcraft pack` |

---

## Important Rules

1. **`containers` + `resources` (oci-image) are the minimum additions** that distinguish a Kubernetes sidecar charm from a machine charm.
2. Every container's `resource:` value **must** reference an entry in `resources` with `type: oci-image`.
3. Every container `mounts[].storage` value **must** reference an entry in `storage`.
4. All of `metadata.yaml`, `config.yaml`, and `actions.yaml` can be **consolidated** into `charmcraft.yaml` (recommended since Charmcraft 2.x) or kept as separate files — but **not both**.
5. The `bases` `channel` must be a quoted string (e.g., `"22.04"`) to prevent YAML from parsing it as a float.
